facts.statement: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) is the molecule that carries genetic instructions in living organisms.
questions: What molecule carries genetic instructions in living organisms?
What does DNA stand for?
What is the function of DNA in living organisms?

facts.statement: DNA has a double helix structure.
questions: What is the structure of DNA?
What shape does DNA have?
What structural form does DNA take?

facts.statement: The double helix structure of DNA was described by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953.
questions: Who described the double helix structure of DNA?
When was the DNA double helix structure described?
Which scientists are credited with describing DNA structure in 1953?

facts.statement: James Watson and Francis Crick built on the X-ray diffraction work of Rosalind Franklin.
questions: Whose X-ray diffraction work did Watson and Crick build upon?
What technique did Rosalind Franklin use to study DNA?
Who contributed X-ray diffraction data to the discovery of DNA structure?

facts.statement: DNA is made of four nucleotide bases: adenine, thymine, guanine, and cytosine.
questions: What are the four bases in DNA?
How many nucleotide bases make up DNA?
What nucleotides comprise DNA?

facts.statement: In DNA, adenine pairs with thymine and guanine pairs with cytosine.
questions: What base does adenine pair with in DNA?
How do the DNA bases pair together?
Which base pairs with guanine in DNA?

facts.statement: The order of DNA bases encodes genetic information.
questions: How does DNA encode genetic information?
What determines the genetic information in DNA?
What role does base order play in DNA?
